headline: Open Bionics rotation re-confirm. May 2 first-baseline assessment computed composite 86.3 vs published 97.5; index-maintenance hygiene flag carried forward (per-dimension means of 4.5 yield composite 87.5, not 97.5). New evidence in this cycle: confirmation that NHS England has standing contract for Hero Arm and Hero Flex including child access; Hero PRO and Hero RGD continue as the most advanced multi-grip pediatric prosthetic stack. No new in-window negative signals. Sub-threshold confirmation; band Exemplary sustained. Maintenance flag carried.
key_evidence: NHS England and Wales contract for Hero Arm and Hero Flex confirmed standing — pediatric activity attachments meet NHS sports/recreation funding criteria.
Hero PRO and Hero RGD launched April 2025 — wireless, waterproof, faster than existing bionic hands; based on feedback from ~1,000 users.
Hero Arm available for children as young as 5 (lowered from 8) — pediatric multi-grip myoelectric leadership confirmed in current materials.
800+ US clinical partner locations through Hanger Clinic.
5x cost reduction vs. traditional bionic prosthetics ($100K) addresses sub-1% prosthetic affordability gap.
Math hygiene: published composite 97.5 inconsistent with per-dimension means of 4.5 (which yields 87.5 via canonical formula). Maintenance flag carried from May 2 cycle.

evidence.source: Open Bionics — When Will The NHS Fund My Hero Arm
evidence.finding: NHS England and Wales contract for Hero Arm and Hero Flex confirmed; pediatric activity attachments funded for children where clinically needed.

evidence.source: Open Bionics — Free NHS Hero Flexes for Children
evidence.finding: NHS England funds activity arms for children meeting NHS criteria — universal pediatric access pathway in UK.
Hero PRO and Hero RGD launched April 2025 — wireless, waterproof, twice as fast as many existing bionic hands.

scoring_rationale.AWR: 4.4. Strong product-feedback awareness; minor gap in publicly disclosed structured suffering-detection for discontinued users.
scoring_rationale.EMP: 4.6. Children co-designing themed covers; 'superpowers' framing; E2 Perspective-Taking strongly evidenced.
scoring_rationale.ACT: 4.5. Hero PRO/RGD launch and pediatric-5 lowering are documented capability expansions.
scoring_rationale.EQU: 4.5. EQ4 Access Design via 5x cost reduction and pediatric-5 availability; EQ2 Priority for Vulnerable embedded structurally.
scoring_rationale.BND: 4.4. B1 Self-Sustainability limited public evidence — UK company scaling globally; minor risk consideration.
scoring_rationale.ACC: 4.2. AB3 Transparency: less public independent evaluation, longitudinal outcome tracking, or failure disclosure than would warrant 4.5.
scoring_rationale.SYS: 4.5. S1 Root Cause Orientation addresses prosthetic-affordability; S5 Coalitional Compassion via clinical partner network.
scoring_rationale.INT: 4.5. I2 Non-Performance: mission-aligned business model with documented children-first design choices.

math_check.validator_check: -11.2 vs published 97.5; this is a math-hygiene gap, not a substantive degradation. Per May 2 cycle: published composite 97.5 implies dimension mean of 4.9 but published per-dimension scores are uniformly 4.5 (composite should be 87.5). Index maintenance review recommended; this proposal does not propose substantive downgrade.

math_hygiene_flag: CARRIED FORWARD from May 2 cycle. Open Bionics published composite 97.5 cannot be reconstructed from published dimension means. Either restore higher dimension means consistent with 97.5, or correct composite to ~87.5. This is an index-maintenance review, not a substantive score change. Confidence-medium until resolved.
negativeSignals: Limited public independent outcome evaluations; limited employee culture data; small-company scaling risks.
positiveSignals: Standing NHS England/Wales contract; pediatric-5 access; 5x cost reduction; community-input design; 800+ US clinical partner locations; 2025 Hero PRO/RGD product expansion; mission-aligned brand consistency.
sector_context: Robotics labs sector context: Healthcare/Accessibility category continues to lead mission-alignment metrics. Math-hygiene gap is sector-isolated to Open Bionics in this cycle.
open_actions_carried: Index maintenance review of Open Bionics published composite (97.5 vs calculated 87.5)
Re-queue with focus on independent outcome evaluation evidence and employee culture in subsequent cycles
